task_completion.description: Access the Direct Foil Design module in QBlade
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QBlade application is open and the 'Direct Foil Design' module is active (visible in the module selection dropdown, toolbar, or window title).
An airfoil shape is visible in the design area with spline control points, and the upper and lower surfaces are mirror images of each other (zero camber).

task_completion.description: Set the maximum thickness to approximately 15% of the chord
task_completion.visual_evidence: The airfoil properties or design parameters show a thickness of ~15% (or 0.15), or the visual proportions of the airfoil clearly show the maximum vertical thickness is about 15% of the horizontal length.
The name 'SymCustom15' is visible in the airfoil list, object explorer, or properties panel within the QBlade interface.

task_completion.description: Save the project to the specified path and filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the directory '/home/ga/Documents/projects/' and the filename 'custom_spline_foil.wpa', or a file manager/terminal confirms the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the QBlade graphical user interface to design the airfoil
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the QBlade GUI (spline manipulation, menus, dialogs) rather than just terminal commands generating a text file.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the Direct Foil Design tools rather than importing an existing airfoil
integrity.visual_evidence: Spline control points or design manipulation tools are visible on the airfoil curve in at least one screenshot.

integrity.description: The project was saved using the application's native save functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: The QBlade save dialog is used, or the main QBlade window title updates to reflect the saved project name 'custom_spline_foil.wpa'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Software: QBlade v0.96 (Linux 64-bit). The 'Direct Foil Design' module is integrated from XFLR5 and uses spline control points. A symmetric airfoil has zero camber (upper and lower surfaces are mirror images). The target thickness is 15% of the chord length. The expected airfoil name is 'SymCustom15'. The project must be saved as a legacy text-based QBlade project file (.wpa) at '/home/ga/Documents/projects/custom_spline_foil.wpa'.
